You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 05a5e229).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

open the browser
Select a card which is not card 1 of the note
long press
in the menu, tap "edit note"
Tap preview

### Expected behaviour

You should see the selected card in the preview mode (or at least, the card should be consistent with the card underlined at the bottom of the editor)

### Actual behaviour

First card of the note is shown (At least I assume. In the case I was interested in, I saw the "this card is empty" warning)

### Debug info

```text
2.17 alpha 3
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This will be slightly easier to fix once https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/14719 goes in:

* `ordinal` is provided to the editor: 
* `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
  * `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L84

`mOrdinal` is not used to set `templateIndex` in this branch:

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L233-L241

`setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` uses `templateIndex = 0` and displays the wrong card

I've added two regression tests to help guide a fix

One undefined question: what should the note editor previewer show if one of the cards is empty? Should it be skipped, or displayed

Should this be different between:
* Card Template Preview
* Note Editor Preview
* Card Browser Preview (less likely to want to see empty cards)

For when a few dependencies have been merged:

```kotlin
    private fun indexFromOrdinal(col: Collection, ordinal: Int): Int {
        return when (mEditedNotetype?.isCloze) {
            true -> col.clozeNumbersInNote(col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!)).indexOf(mOrdinal)
            else -> ordinal
        }
    }
```
Is that correct? with c1 and c3, that will return [1,3], and ord 3 will be an index out of bounds, won't it?

Edit: sorry, ignore me - I mistook this for numbers[ord] instead of searching for the given value.
In the note editor preview, there seems to be no specific card. So I don’t understand what sense it would make to have this behavior on the note editor preview.

For card template preview, I certainly believe you should see the preview for the card you are looking it.

For the browser, if the card selected in the browser is empty, I believe you should show that the card is empty. That’s an information I certainly may want to have if it is the case. Because either:
* there was no recent "clean card"
* there was a clear card, and this is card 1, generated in order to ensure the note is not deleted. 

The first case may be interesting, the second is certainly something that should be noted.

Also, if we touch this part of the code, it may be worth to give more context, by stating either:
* This card is empty. The n cards of this note are empty.
* This card is empty. Jump to the first non-empty card. (with a link/button)

And I guess, potentially, a checkbox to skip the empty card when browsing which cards will be generated.


If you agree it makes sense, I assume it should be many different bugs so they can be easily tackled

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 36→import com.ichi2.libanki.exception.ConfirmModSchemaException
 37→import com.ichi2.libanki.exception.InvalidSearchException
 38→import com.ichi2.libanki.sched.DummyScheduler
 39→import com.ichi2.libanki.sched.Scheduler
 40→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.Time
 41→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.TimeManager
 42→import com.ichi2.utils.*
 43→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend
 44→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
 45→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.exceptions.BackendInvalidInputException
 46→import timber.log.Timber
 47→import java.io.*
 48→import java.util.*
 49→
 50→// Anki maintains a cache of used tags so it can quickly present a list of tags
 51→// for autocomplete and in the browser. For efficiency, deletions are not
 52→// tracked, so unused tags can only be removed from the list with a DB check.
 53→//
 54→// This module manages the tag cache and tags for notes.
 55→@KotlinCleanup("TextUtils -> Kotlin isNotEmpty()")
 56→@KotlinCleanup("inline function in init { } so we don't need to init `crt` etc... at the definition")
 57→@KotlinCleanup("ids.size != 0")
 58→@WorkerThread
 59→open class Collection(
 60→    /**
 61→     *  @param Path The path to the collection.anki2 database. Must be unicode and openable with [File].
 62→     */
 63→    val path: String,
 64→    private var debugLog: Boolean, // Not in libAnki.
 65→    /**
 66→     * Outside of libanki, you should not access the backend directly for collection operations.
 67→     * Operations that work on a closed collection (eg importing), or do not require a collection
 68→     * at all (eg translations) are the exception.
 69→     */
 70→    val backend: Backend
 71→) {
 72→    /** Access backend translations */
 73→    val tr = backend.tr
 74→
 75→    @get:JvmName("isDbClosed")
 76→    val dbClosed: Boolean
 77→        get() {
 78→            return dbInternal == null
 79→        }
 80→
 81→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 82→    fun debugEnsureNoOpenPointers() {
 83→        val result = backend.getActiveSequenceNumbers()
 84→        if (result.isNotEmpty()) {
 85→            val numbers = result.toString()
 86→            throw IllegalStateException("Contained unclosed sequence numbers: $numbers")
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    // a lot of legacy code does not check for nullability
 91→    val db: DB
 92→        get() = dbInternal!!
 93→
 94→    var dbInternal: DB? = null
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * Getters/Setters ********************************************************** *************************************
 98→     */
 99→
100→    val media: Media
101→
102→    lateinit var decks: Decks
103→        protected set
104→
105→    val tags: Tags
106→
107→    @KotlinCleanup(
108→        "move accessor methods here, maybe reconsider return type." +
109→            "See variable: conf"
110→    )
111→    lateinit var config: Config
112→
113→    @KotlinCleanup("see if we can inline a function inside init {} and make this `val`")
114→    lateinit var sched: Scheduler
115→        protected set
116→
117→    private var mStartTime: Long
118→    private var mStartReps: Int
119→
120→    var mod: Long = 0
121→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select mod from col")
122→
123→    var crt: Long = 0
124→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select crt from col")
125→
126→    var scm: Long = 0
127→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select scm from col")
128→
129→    var lastSync: Long = 0
130→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select ls from col")
131→
132→    fun usn(): Int {
133→        return -1
134→    }
135→
136→    var ls: Long = 0
137→    // END: SQL table columns
138→
139→    private var mLogHnd: PrintWriter? = null
140→
141→    init {
142→        media = initMedia()
143→        tags = initTags()
144→        val created = reopen()
145→        log(path, VersionUtils.pkgVersionName)
146→        mStartReps = 0
147→        mStartTime = 0
148→        _loadScheduler()
149→        if (created) {
150→            onCreate()
151→        }
152→    }
153→
154→    protected open fun initMedia(): Media {
155→        return Media(this)
156→    }
157→
158→    protected open fun initDecks(): Decks {
159→        return Decks(this)
160→    }
161→
162→    protected open fun initConf(): Config {
163→        return Config(backend)
164→    }
165→
166→    protected open fun initTags(): Tags {
167→        return Tags(this)
168→    }
169→
170→    protected open fun initModels(): Notetypes {
171→        return Notetypes(this)
172→    }
173→
174→    fun name(): String {
175→        // TODO:
176→        return File(path).name.replace(".anki2", "")
177→    }
178→
179→    /**
180→     * Scheduler
181→     * ***********************************************************
182→     */
183→    fun schedVer(): Int {
184→        // schedVer was not set on legacy v1 collections
185→        val ver = config.get("schedVer") ?: 1
186→        return if (listOf(1, 2).contains(ver)) {
187→            ver
188→        } else {
189→            throw RuntimeException("Unsupported scheduler version")
190→        }
191→    }
192→
193→    fun _loadScheduler() {
194→        val ver = schedVer()
195→        if (ver == 1) {
196→            sched = DummyScheduler(this)
197→        } else {
198→            if (!backend.getConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021)) {
199→                backend.setConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021, true, undoable = false)
200→            }
201→            sched = Scheduler(this)
202→            config.set("localOffset", sched._current_timezone_offset())
203→        }
204→    }
205→
206→    /**
207→     * Disconnect from DB.
208→     * Python implementation has a save argument for legacy reasons;
209→     * AnkiDroid always saves as changes are made.
210→     */
211→    @Synchronized
212→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
213→        if (!dbClosed) {
214→            if (!forFullSync) {
215→                backend.closeCollection(downgrade)
216→            }
217→            dbInternal = null
218→            _closeLog()
219→            Timber.i("Collection closed")
220→        }
221→    }
222→
223→    /** True if DB was created */
224→    fun reopen(afterFullSync: Boolean = false): Boolean {
225→        Timber.i("(Re)opening Database: %s", path)
226→        return if (dbClosed) {
227→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
228→            dbInternal = db_
229→            load()
230→            _openLog()
231→            if (afterFullSync) {
232→                _loadScheduler()
233→            }
234→            created
235→        } else {
236→            false
237→        }
238→    }
239→
240→    fun load() {
241→        notetypes = initModels()
242→        decks = initDecks()
243→        config = initConf()
244→    }
245→
246→    /** Note: not in libanki.  Mark schema modified to force a full
247→     * sync, but with the confirmation checking function disabled This
248→     * is equivalent to `modSchema(False)` in Anki. A distinct method
249→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
250→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.
251→     */
252→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {
253→        db.execute(
254→            "update col set scm=?, mod=?",
255→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS(),
256→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS()
257→        )
258→    }
259→
260→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
261→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
262→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
263→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
264→     *
265→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
266→     */
267→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
268→    fun modSchema() {
269→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
270→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
271→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
272→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
273→             code to handle the user's choice */
274→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
275→        }
276→        modSchemaNoCheck()
277→    }
278→
279→    /** True if schema changed since last sync.  */
280→    fun schemaChanged(): Boolean {
281→        return scm > lastSync
282→    }
283→
284→    /**
285→     * Object creation helpers **************************************************
286→     * *********************************************
287→     */
288→    fun getCard(id: Long): Card {
289→        return Card(this, id)
290→    }
291→
292→    fun getNote(id: Long): Note {
293→        return Note(this, id)
294→    }
295→
296→    /**
297→     * Notes ******************************************************************** ***************************
298→     */
299→    fun noteCount(): Int {
300→        return db.queryScalar("SELECT count() FROM notes")
301→    }
302→
303→    /**
304→     * Return a new note with the model derived from the deck or the configuration
305→     * @param forDeck When true it uses the model specified in the deck (mid), otherwise it uses the model specified in
306→     * the configuration (curModel)
307→     * @return The new note
308→     */
309→    fun newNote(forDeck: Boolean = true): Note {
310→        return newNote(notetypes.current(forDeck))
311→    }
312→
313→    /**
314→     * Return a new note with a specific model
315→     * @param m The model to use for the new note
316→     * @return The new note
317→     */
318→    fun newNote(m: NotetypeJson): Note {
319→        return Note(this, m)
320→    }
321→
322→    /**
323→     * Cards ******************************************************************** ***************************
324→     */
325→    val isEmpty: Boolean
326→        get() = db.queryScalar("SELECT 1 FROM cards LIMIT 1") == 0
327→
328→    fun cardCount(): Int {
329→        return db.queryScalar("SELECT count() FROM cards")
330→    }
331→
332→    // NOT IN LIBANKI //
333→    fun cardCount(vararg dids: Long): Int {
334→        return db.queryScalar("SELECT count() FROM cards WHERE did IN " + Utils.ids2str(dids))
335→    }
336→
337→    fun isEmptyDeck(vararg dids: Long): Boolean {
338→        return cardCount(*dids) == 0
339→    }
340→
341→    /*
342→      Finding cards ************************************************************ ***********************************
343→     */
344→
345→    /**
346→     * Construct a search string from the provided search nodes. For example:
347→     * */
348→    /*
349→            import anki.search.searchNode
350→            import anki.search.SearchNode
351→            import anki.search.SearchNodeKt.group
352→
353→            val node = searchNode {
354→                group = SearchNodeKt.group {
355→                    joiner = SearchNode.Group.Joiner.AND
356→                    nodes += searchNode { deck = "a **test** deck" }
357→                    nodes += searchNode {
358→                        negated = searchNode {
359→                            tag = "foo"
360→                        }
361→                    }
362→                    nodes += searchNode { flag = SearchNode.Flag.FLAG_GREEN }
363→                }
364→            }
365→            // yields "deck:a \*\*test\*\* deck" -tag:foo flag:3
366→            val text = col.buildSearchString(node)
367→        }
368→    */
369→    fun buildSearchString(node: SearchNode): String {
370→        return backend.buildSearchString(node)
371→    }
372→
373→    fun findCards(
374→        search: String,
375→        order: SortOrder
376→    ): List<Long> {
377→        val adjustedOrder = if (order is SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering) {
378→            SortOrder.BuiltinSortKind(
379→                config.get("sortType") ?: "noteFld",
380→                config.get("sortBackwards") ?: false
381→            )
382→        } else {
383→            order
384→        }
385→        val cardIdsList = try {
386→            backend.searchCards(search, adjustedOrder.toProtoBuf())
387→        } catch (e: BackendInvalidInputException) {
388→            throw InvalidSearchException(e)
389→        }
390→        return cardIdsList
391→    }
392→
393→    fun findNotes(
394→        query: String,
395→        order: SortOrder = SortOrder.NoOrdering()
396→    ): List<Long> {
397→        val adjustedOrder = if (order is SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering) {
398→            SortOrder.BuiltinSortKind(
399→                config.get("noteSortType") ?: "noteFld",
400→                config.get("browserNoteSortBackwards") ?: false
401→            )
402→        } else {
403→            order
404→        }
405→        val noteIDsList = try {
406→            backend.searchNotes(query, adjustedOrder.toProtoBuf())
407→        } catch (e: BackendInvalidInputException) {
408→            throw InvalidSearchException(e)
409→        }
410→        return noteIDsList
411→    }
412→
413→    /** Return a list of card ids  */
414→    @KotlinCleanup("set reasonable defaults")
415→    fun findCards(search: String): List<Long> {
416→        return findCards(search, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
417→    }
418→
419→    /** Return a list of card ids  */
420→    @RustCleanup("Remove in V16.") // Not in libAnki
421→    fun findOneCardByNote(query: String): List<CardId> {
422→        // This function shouldn't exist and CardBrowser should be modified to use Notes,
423→        // so not much effort was expended here
424→
425→        val noteIds = findNotes(query, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
426→        // select the card with the lowest `ord` to show
427→        return db.queryLongList(
428→            """
429→SELECT c.id
430→FROM (
431→  SELECT nid, MIN(ord) AS ord
432→  FROM cards
433→  WHERE nid IN ${Utils.ids2str(noteIds)} 
434→  GROUP BY nid
435→) AS card_with_min_ord

462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

546→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
547→     */
548→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
549→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
550→        // cards without notes
551→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
555→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
556→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
557→        ) > 0
558→        // notes without cards or models
559→        if (badNotes) {
560→            return false
561→        }
562→        // invalid ords
563→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
564→            // ignore clozes
565→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
566→                continue
567→            }
568→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
569→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
570→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
571→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
572→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
573→                tmpls.length(),
574→                m.getLong("id")
575→            ) > 0
576→            if (badOrd) {
577→                return false
578→            }
579→        }
580→        return true
581→    }

583→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
584→        if (!debugLog) return
585→
586→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
587→
588→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
589→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
590→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
591→
592→        val objectsString = objects
593→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
594→            .joinToString(", ")
595→
596→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
597→    }
598→
599→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
600→        mLogHnd?.let {
601→            try {
602→                it.println(s)
603→            } catch (e: Exception) {
604→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
605→            }
606→        }
607→        Timber.d(s)
608→    }
609→
610→    private fun _openLog() {
611→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
612→        if (!debugLog) {
613→            return
614→        }
615→        try {
616→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
617→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
618→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
619→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
620→                    lpath2.delete()
621→                }
622→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
623→            }
624→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
625→        } catch (e: IOException) {
626→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
627→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
628→            debugLog = false
629→        }
630→    }
631→
632→    private fun _closeLog() {
633→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
634→        mLogHnd?.close()
635→        mLogHnd = null
636→    }

content.thinking:
**Evaluating ordinals for Anki cards**

I'm thinking that ordinals for Anki cards might be 0 and 2, since card.ord equals c-1. So if c1 is ord 0 and c3 is ord 2, it confirms that the backend is returning ords instead of cloze numbers. In Note.cardIndexToOrd, using list[index] -1 seems wrong if the list contains ords. However, maybe the backend does return 1-based cloze numbers. I’ll need to cross-check how clozeNumbersInNote is used elsewhere.

403→    private fun assertTwoCards(testCardTemplatePreviewer: TestCardTemplatePreviewer) {
404→        assertThat("prev should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
405→        assertThat("next should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
406→
407→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onNextCard()
408→
409→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
410→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
411→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
412→
413→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onNextCard()
414→
415→        // no effect
416→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
417→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
418→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
419→
420→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onPreviousCard()
421→
422→        // previous
423→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(0))
424→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
425→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
426→    }

428→    private fun getSavedCard(notetype: NotetypeJson, ordinal: Int): Card {
429→        val n = col.newNote(notetype)
430→        val fieldNames = notetype.fieldsNames
431→        for (i in fieldNames.indices) {
432→            n.setField(i, fieldNames[i])
433→        }
434→        col.addNote(n)
435→        print(ordinal)
436→        return n.cards()[0]
437→    }
438→
439→    @KotlinCleanup("Override fieldText in constructor and remove text")
440→    private inner class Field(override val ord: Int, private val text: String) : NoteService.NoteField {
441→        override val fieldText: String
442→            get() = text
443→    }
444→}

21→class TestCardTemplatePreviewer : CardTemplatePreviewer() {

23→        private set
24→
25→    fun disableDoubleClickPrevention() {
26→        lastClickTime = (
27→            baseContext.sharedPrefs()
28→                .getInt(DOUBLE_TAP_TIME_INTERVAL, DEFAULT_DOUBLE_TAP_TIME_INTERVAL) * -2
29→            ).toLong()

38→        super.displayCardQuestion()
39→        showingAnswer = false
40→    }
41→
42→    fun nextButtonVisible(): Boolean {
43→        return previewLayout!!.nextCard.visibility != View.GONE
44→    }
45→
46→    fun previousButtonVisible(): Boolean {
47→        return previewLayout!!.prevCard.visibility != View.GONE
48→    }
49→
50→    fun previousButtonEnabled(): Boolean {
51→        return previewLayout!!.prevCard.isEnabled && previewLayout!!.prevCard.visibility == View.VISIBLE
52→    }
53→
54→    fun nextButtonEnabled(): Boolean {
55→        return previewLayout!!.nextCard.isEnabled && previewLayout!!.nextCard.visibility == View.VISIBLE
56→    }
57→}

 42→import kotlin.test.junit5.JUnit5Asserter.assertTrue
 43→
 44→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
 45→class CardTemplateEditorTest : RobolectricTest() {
 46→    @Test
 47→    @Throws(Exception::class)
 48→    fun testEditTemplateContents() {
 49→        val modelName = "Basic"
 50→
 51→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
 52→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
 53→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 54→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
 55→        var templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 56→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 57→        var testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 58→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed yet", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 59→
 60→        // Change the model and make sure it registers as changed, but the database is unchanged
 61→        var templateFront = testEditor.findViewById<EditText>(R.id.editor_editText)
 62→        val testModelQfmtEdit = "!@#$%^&*TEST*&^%$#@!"
 63→        templateFront.text.append(testModelQfmtEdit)
 64→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 65→        assertTrue("Model did not change after edit?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 66→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
 67→
 68→        // Kill and restart the Activity, make sure model edit is preserved
 69→        val outBundle = Bundle()
 70→        templateEditorController.saveInstanceState(outBundle)
 71→        templateEditorController.pause().stop().destroy()
 72→        templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java).create(outBundle).start().resume().visible()
 73→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 74→        testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 75→        var shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
 76→        assertTrue("model change not preserved across activity lifecycle?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 77→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
 78→
 79→        // Make sure we get a confirmation dialog if we hit the back button
 80→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home))
 81→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 82→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes))
 83→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_NEGATIVE, false)
 84→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 85→        assertTrue("model change not preserved despite canceling back button?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 86→
 87→        // Make sure we things are cleared out after a cancel
 88→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home))
 89→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes))
 90→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, false)
 91→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 92→        assertFalse("model change not cleared despite discarding changes?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 93→
 94→        // Get going for content edit assertions again...
 95→        templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 96→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 97→        testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 98→        shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
 99→        templateFront = testEditor.findViewById(R.id.editor_editText)
100→        templateFront.text.append(testModelQfmtEdit)
101→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
102→        assertTrue("Model did not change after edit?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
103→
104→        // Make sure we pass the edit to the Previewer
105→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_preview))
106→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
107→        val startedIntent = shadowTestEditor.nextStartedActivity
108→        val shadowIntent = shadowOf(startedIntent)
109→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
110→        assertEquals("Previewer not started?", CardTemplatePreviewer::class.java.name, shadowIntent.intentClass.name)
111→        assertNotNull("intent did not have model JSON filename?", startedIntent.getStringExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME))
112→        assertNotEquals("Model sent to Previewer is unchanged?", testEditor.tempModel?.notetype, CardTemplateNotetype.getTempModel(startedIntent.getStringExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME)!!))
113→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
114→        shadowTestEditor.receiveResult(startedIntent, Activity.RESULT_OK, Intent())
115→
116→        // Save the template then fetch it from the collection to see if it was saved correctly
117→        val testEditorModelEdited = testEditor.tempModel?.notetype
118→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
119→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_confirm))
120→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
121→        val collectionBasicModelCopyEdited = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
122→        assertNotEquals("model is unchanged?", collectionBasicModelOriginal, collectionBasicModelCopyEdited)
123→        assertEquals("model did not save?", testEditorModelEdited.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, collectionBasicModelCopyEdited.toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
124→        assertTrue("model does not have our change?", collectionBasicModelCopyEdited.toString().contains(testModelQfmtEdit))
125→    }

214→    /**
215→     * In a model with two card templates using different fields, some notes may only use card 1,
216→     * and some may only use card 2. If you delete the 2nd template,
217→     * it will cause the notes that only use card 2 to disappear.
218→     *
219→     * So the unit test would then be to make a model like the "basic (optional reverse card)"
220→     * with two fields Enable1 and Enable2, and two templates "card 1" and "card 2".
221→     * Both cards use selective generation, so they're empty unless the corresponding field is set.
222→     *
223→     * So then in the unit test you make the model, add the two templates, then you add two notes,
224→     * with Enable1 and Enable2 respectively set to "y".
225→     * Then you try to delete one of the templates and it should fail
226→     *
227→     * (question: but I thought deleting one should work - still one card left to maintain the note,
228→     * and second template delete should fail since we finally get to a place where no cards are left?
229→     * I am having trouble creating selectively generated cards though - I can do one optional field but not 2 ugh)
230→     *
231→     */
232→    @Test
233→    fun testDeleteTemplateWithSelectivelyGeneratedCards() {
234→        val modelName = "Basic (optional reversed card)"
235→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
236→
237→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
238→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
239→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
240→        val templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
241→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
242→        val testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
243→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed yet", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
244→        assertEquals("Model should have 2 templates now", 2, testEditor.tempModel?.templateCount)
245→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 0))
246→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 1))
247→
248→        // Try to delete Card 1 template - click delete, check confirm for card delete popup indicating it was possible, then dismiss it
249→        val shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
250→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_delete))
251→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
252→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", "Delete the “Card 1” card type, and its 0 cards?", getMaterialDialogText(true))
253→        clickMaterialDialogButton(WhichButton.NEGATIVE, true)
254→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
255→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
256→
257→        // Create note with forward and back info, Add Reverse is empty, so should only be one card
258→        val selectiveGeneratedNote = col.newNote(collectionBasicModelOriginal)
259→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(0, "TestFront")
260→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(1, "TestBack")
261→        val fields = selectiveGeneratedNote.fields
262→        for (field in fields) {
263→            Timber.d("Got a field: %s", field)
264→        }
265→        col.addNote(selectiveGeneratedNote)
266→        assertEquals("selective generation should result in one card", 1, getModelCardCount(collectionBasicModelOriginal))
267→
268→        // Try to delete the template again, but there's selective generation means it would orphan the note
269→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_delete))
270→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
271→        assertEquals(
272→            "Did not show dialog about deleting only card?",
273→            getResourceString(R.string.card_template_editor_would_delete_note),
274→            getAlertDialogText(true)
275→        )
276→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, true)
277→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
278→        assertNull("Can delete used template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
279→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
280→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 0))
281→        assertEquals("Change incorrectly added to list?", 0, testEditor.tempModel?.templateChanges?.size)
282→
283→        // Assert can delete 'Card 2'
284→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete unused template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
285→
286→        // Edit note to have Add Reverse set to 'y' so we get a second card
287→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(2, "y")
288→        selectiveGeneratedNote.flush()
289→
290→        // - assert two cards
291→        assertEquals("should be two cards now", 2, getModelCardCount(collectionBasicModelOriginal))
292→
293→        // - assert can delete either Card template but not both
294→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
295→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
296→        assertNull("Can delete both templates?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0, 1)))
297→
298→        // A couple more notes to make sure things are okay
299→        val secondNote = col.newNote(collectionBasicModelOriginal)
300→        secondNote.setField(0, "TestFront2")
301→        secondNote.setField(1, "TestBack2")
302→        secondNote.setField(2, "y")
303→        col.addNote(secondNote)
304→
305→        // - assert can delete either Card template but not both
306→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
307→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
308→        assertNull("Can delete both templates?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0, 1)))
309→    }

591→    private fun addCardType(testEditor: CardTemplateEditor, shadowTestEditor: ShadowActivity) {
592→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_add))
593→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
594→        val ordinal = testEditor.viewPager.currentItem
595→        val numAffectedCards = if (!CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, ordinal)) {
596→            col.notetypes.tmplUseCount(testEditor.tempModel!!.notetype, ordinal)
597→        } else {
598→            0
599→        }
600→        assertEquals(
601→            "Did not show dialog about adding template and it's card?",
602→            getQuantityString(R.plurals.card_template_editor_confirm_add, numAffectedCards, numAffectedCards),
603→            getMaterialDialogText(true)
604→        )
605→        clickMaterialDialogButton(WhichButton.POSITIVE, true)
606→    }
607→
608→    private fun getModelCardCount(notetype: NotetypeJson): Int {
609→        var cardCount = 0
610→        for (noteId in col.notetypes.nids(notetype)) {
611→            cardCount += col.getNote(noteId).numberOfCards()
612→        }
613→        return cardCount
614→    }
615→}

**Evaluating test implementation**

I see that we have some tests related to issue 14694 which are currently ignored and might be hidden tests. We need to consider implementing those and possibly unignoring them. While I can't modify existing tests, I can add new ones in separate files. Hidden tests should verify the logic we implement, but I can also create additional scenarios if needed. As for the mOrdinal, it’s passed in extras and can come from card.ord or template index for different note types, and I realize it indicates ord = 2.**Implementing note editor logic**

In the note editor previewer, I should start from the chosen card ordinal, mapping it to the index of valid cards, specifically for cloze notes. In standard notes, the index corresponds directly to the ordinal as long as it's within range. To be safe, I’ll implement an indexFromOrdinal function and use it in onCollectionLoaded when noteEditorBundle isn't null, setting cardIndex accordingly. 

Also, I need to ensure that the previewLayout navigation buttons are enabled or disabled based on the cardCount from toPreview.note().numberOfCardsEphemeral(). This uses the size of the clozeNumbersInNote list, but it must consider the ephemeral note context rather than the newNote model. The initial index mapping is essential to render the correct card, and this mapping depends on the bundle fields. So, we can create a new note and compute the cloze numbers as needed.**Mapping card indices**

I’m thinking about how to create a mapping by constructing a dummy note with fields so I can compute the index. It seems easiest to do this in onCollectionLoaded when the noteEditorBundle isn’t null. I want to determine the initial cardIndex using the provided mOrdinal and the note-card mapping. 

To do this, I plan to create a note from mEditedNotetype, fill it from the bundle, and then use the indexFromOrd method for mapping ord to index. I know there are existing methods like Note.cardIndexToOrd that involve adjustments, but I need to confirm the actual representation to avoid off-by-one errors. It seems that with clozeNumbersInNote returning lists, understanding the structure is key to ensuring everything works correctly. The tests do provide some assurance that it’s functioning as expected for the card count and navigation.**Clarifying cloze mapping**

So, I’m gathering that the col.clozeNumbersInNote function returns cloze numbers as 1-based, like [1,3] for c1 and c3. The cardIndexToOrd method converts those back to a 0-based index, which seems consistent with the comments in Collection.kt being incorrect. 

If clozeNumbersInNote indeed returns [1,3], then the size of numberOfCardsEphemeral is correctly represented, and cardIndexToOrd yields ord values of 0,2. The good news is that using cloze templates aligns everything well. 

To map ord values to cardIndex, I’ll convert ord to index by using indexOf(ord + 1) since the list returns cloze numbers. The earlier suggestion in the discussion reflects this, but I need to make sure to avoid confusion with direct usage of mOrdinal.**Implementing note preview logic**

I’m planning to create a private function for building a Note for previews. In onCollectionLoaded, I think I can initialize it using col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!) and fill the fields from the bundle. By calculating cardCount directly, I hope to simplify things without pre-creating a currentCard.

I will manage cases where mEditedNotetype might be null and ensure that we clamp cardIndex to valid ranges to avoid any out-of-bounds errors. Additionally, cardCount will vary based on note types. I also realize I need to address how ordinal values relate to card display in tests, particularly for cloze templates, confirming they reveal the correct content. 

However, I still need to check if the cardContent property exists in our test setup!

234→            if (!mBackground) {
235→                return

238→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).idle()

244→         * * Executes all posted tasks scheduled before or at the current time
245→         *
246→         * Supersedes and will eventually replace [advanceRobolectricLooper] and [advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep]
247→         */
248→        fun advanceRobolectricUiLooper() {
249→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).apply {
250→                runToEndOfTasks()
251→                idle()
252→                // CardBrowserTest:browserIsInMultiSelectModeWhenSelectingAll failed on Windows CI

258→        // Robolectric needs some help sometimes in form of a manual kick, then a wait, to stabilize UI activity
259→        fun advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() {
260→            if (!mBackground) {
261→                return
262→            }
263→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
264→            try {

266→            } catch (e: Exception) {
267→                Timber.e(e)
268→            }

393→    protected fun addNoteUsingModelName(name: String?, vararg fields: String): Note {

396→        // PERF: if we modify newNote(), we can return the card and return a Pair<Note, Card> here.

576→    /** * A wrapper around the standard [kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest] that
577→     * takes care of updating the dispatcher used by CollectionManager as well.
578→     * * An argument could be made for using [StandardTestDispatcher] and
579→     * explicitly advanced coroutines with advanceUntilIdle(), but there are
580→     * issues with using it at the moment:
581→     * * - Any usage of CollectionManager with runBlocking() will hang. tearDown()
582→     * calls runBlocking() twice, which prevents tests from finishing.
583→     * - The hang is not limited to the scope of runTest(). Even if the runBlocking
584→     * calls in tearDown() are selectively moved into this function,
585→     * when a coroutine test fails, the next regular test
586→     * that executes after it will call runBlocking(), and it then hangs.
587→     *
588→     * A fix for this might require either wrapping all tests in runTest(),
589→     * or finding some other way to isolate the coroutine and non-coroutine tests
590→     * on separate threads/processes.
591→     * */
592→    fun runTest(
593→        context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
594→        dispatchTimeoutMs: Long = 60_000L,
595→        testBody: suspend TestScope.() -> Unit
596→    ) {
597→        Dispatchers.setMain(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())
598→        runTest(context, dispatchTimeoutMs.milliseconds) {
599→            CollectionManager.setTestDispatcher(UnconfinedTestDispatcher(testScheduler))
600→            testBody()
601→        }
602→    }
603→}

content.thinking:
**Adjusting test assertions**

I need to check if `data-ordinal="3"` appears and if the "cloze-inactive" for Hello is ordinal 1 while cloze for World is ordinal 3. Selecting c3 means that the cloze class for data-ordinal 3 must be present. So, I should adjust the test to assert `body class="card card3"`. I'll modify the cloze test to check if it contains "card3" and also assert for `data-ordinal=\"3\"`. It seems safe to keep the "Hello" visibility check as well.**Editing test assertions**

I'm planning to edit the test file to replace the existing assertion: `assertThat("World should be hidden ...", previewer.cardContent, not(containsString("World")))` with new assertions. 

The new assertions will be: `assertThat("The selected card should be the c3 card", previewer.cardContent, containsString("card3"))` and `assertThat("The selected cloze ordinal should be 3", previewer.cardContent, containsString("data-ordinal=\"3\""))`. 

In Kotlin, I can use `containsString("data-ordinal=\"3\"")` to streamline the check. Let’s go ahead and apply these changes!

function.name: edit
function.arguments:
{"file_path":"/project/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewerOrdinalTest.kt","old_string":"        assertThat("Hello should be visible on the c3 card", previewer.cardContent, containsString("Hello"))
        assertThat("World should be hidden on the c3 card", previewer.cardContent, not(containsString("World")))
","new_string":"        assertThat("Hello should be visible on the c3 card", previewer.cardContent, containsString("Hello"))
        // Ensure the selected card is the card with ordinal 2 ("Card 3" in the HTML output)
        assertThat("The selected card should be the c3 card", previewer.cardContent, containsString("card3"))
        assertThat("The selected cloze ordinal should be 3", previewer.cardContent, containsString("data-ordinal=\"3\""))
"}

17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
18→
19→import android.content.Context
20→import android.view.View
21→import android.widget.FrameLayout
22→import android.widget.ImageView
23→import android.widget.TextView
24→import androidx.annotation.StringRes
25→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
26→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
27→import com.ichi2.anki.R
28→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
29→
30→/** The "preview" controls at the bottom of the screen for [com.ichi2.anki.Previewer] and [com.ichi2.anki.CardTemplatePreviewer] */
31→class PreviewLayout(
32→    private val buttonsLayout: FrameLayout,
33→    @VisibleForTesting val prevCard: ImageView,
34→    @VisibleForTesting val nextCard: ImageView,
35→    private val toggleAnswerText: TextView
36→) {
37→    fun displayAndInit(toggleAnswerHandler: View.OnClickListener) {
38→        buttonsLayout.visibility = View.VISIBLE
39→        buttonsLayout.setOnClickListener(toggleAnswerHandler)
40→    }
41→
42→    /** Sets the answer text to "show answer" or "hide answer" */
43→    fun setShowingAnswer(showingAnswer: Boolean) =
44→        setToggleAnswerText(if (showingAnswer) R.string.hide_answer else R.string.show_answer)
45→
46→    fun setToggleAnswerText(@StringRes res: Int) = toggleAnswerText.setText(res)
47→
48→    fun setOnPreviousCard(listener: View.OnClickListener) = prevCard.setOnClickListener(listener)
49→    fun setOnNextCard(listener: View.OnClickListener) = nextCard.setOnClickListener(listener)
50→
51→    fun setNextButtonEnabled(enabled: Boolean) = setEnabled(nextCard, enabled)
52→    fun setPrevButtonEnabled(enabled: Boolean) = setEnabled(prevCard, enabled)
53→
54→    private fun setEnabled(button: ImageView, enabled: Boolean) {
55→        button.isEnabled = enabled
56→        button.alpha = if (enabled) 1f else 0.38f
57→    }
58→
59→    fun enableAnimation(context: Context) {
60→        val resId = Themes.getResFromAttr(context, R.attr.hardButtonRippleRef)
61→        buttonsLayout.setBackgroundResource(resId)
62→        prevCard.setBackgroundResource(R.drawable.item_background_light_selectable_borderless)
63→        nextCard.setBackgroundResource(R.drawable.item_background_light_selectable_borderless)
64→    }
65→
66→    fun hideNavigationButtons() {
67→        prevCard.visibility = View.GONE
68→        nextCard.visibility = View.GONE
69→    }
70→
71→    fun showNavigationButtons() {
72→        prevCard.visibility = View.VISIBLE
73→        nextCard.visibility = View.VISIBLE
74→    }
75→
76→    companion object {
77→        fun createAndDisplay(activity: AnkiActivity, toggleAnswerHandler: View.OnClickListener): PreviewLayout {
78→            val buttonsLayout = activity.findViewById<FrameLayout>(R.id.preview_buttons_layout)
79→            val prevCard = activity.findViewById<ImageView>(R.id.preview_previous_flashcard)
80→            val nextCard = activity.findViewById<ImageView>(R.id.preview_next_flashcard)
81→            val toggleAnswerText = activity.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.preview_flip_flashcard)
82→
83→            val previewLayout = PreviewLayout(buttonsLayout, prevCard, nextCard, toggleAnswerText)
84→            previewLayout.displayAndInit(toggleAnswerHandler)
85→            if (activity.animationEnabled()) {
86→                previewLayout.enableAnimation(activity)
87→            }
88→            return previewLayout
89→        }
90→    }
91→}

content.thinking:
**Analyzing card navigation**

I'm realizing that the order of the cardIndex list is crucial for navigation. In the existing code, numberOfCardsEphemeral just considers the list size. When onNextCard increments the index, cardIndexToOrd uses list[index] - 1. If the list order is unsorted, navigation follows that order. In the test for Note Editor Cloze preview, starting cardIndex at 0 should show the second cloze, but there are inconsistencies with expected outputs and assertions regarding visible content.**Reviewing card assertions**

I'm considering the c1 card, which has the data-cloze attribute of "Hello." This allows the assertion that the second card contains "Hello" to pass, indicating that the tests might not be robust. The list order could be [3,1], allowing old tests to still function. The fix should involve mapping based on the ordinal within the current order. I think navigation should start at the card matching the selected ordinal, which suggests adjustments in how we handle the cardIndex. The navigation order might need to be sorted by ordinal for proper preview in the note editor.**Addressing card order issues**

I've realized that if the backend returns an unsorted list, there might be a mismatch when expecting the "first" card to be c1. The main issue revolves around mapping the selected card (ordinal). Ideally, c1 should be first, but with an unsorted list, its cardIndex is 1, meaning it's not first. To resolve this, I think we should sort the cloze numbers list in ascending order before using it for indexing. This change will help maintain predictable navigation and satisfy existing tests expecting certain behaviors, but first, we should confirm the actual backend ordering.**Examining cloze number order**

I'm contemplating that the list might return ordinals [0,2] in sorted order, but I'm not entirely sure. The output from the cloze test indicates that body class card3 corresponds to c3 selection, with `data-ordinal="3"` for clozeNumber 3. It shows cloze-inactive with `data-ordinal="1"` for "Hello." This suggests cloze numbers are 1 and 3, but it doesn't clarify their order. 

I think we might need to compute clozeNumbersInNote in a test setup. Alternatively, we can adjust getInitialCardIndexFromOrdinal to use sorted cloze numbers, ensuring that cardIndex 0 aligns with the lowest ordinal. This way, both mapping and rendering through EphemeralCard.cardIndexToOrd stay consistent.**Evaluating backend sorting**

I’m considering if the backend is returning data sorted in descending order, which could explain why the old tests are passing due to non-robust assertions, but that would be confusing for the user experience. If it’s truly sorted ascending, our mapping returning 1 for c1 shouldn’t occur, but it did. 

I realize we need to validate the order of cloze numbers so implementing sorting during mapping for the initial index and in cardIndexToOrd seems necessary. It's also essential to ensure consistency in how these card indices work, especially since our navigation could mislead users if we don’t verify it properly. I’ll implement the changes in the suggested methods carefully to maintain stability and clarity.

**Analyzing ordinal issues**

I'm thinking that clozeNumbersInNote probably returns ordinals in a 0-based format, while cardIndexToOrd should be using the list at the given index, so subtracting 1 would be incorrect. However, if the list sometimes returns values like [1,3], then that creates confusion. I noticed the HTML for card1 has spans showing a cloze number of 1 instead of ord. This means the ord number relates differently than I initially thought. 

If ord is 0, the corresponding body class is card1, suggesting that I may need to rethink the list of ords here, especially considering potential clamping with card assignments.**Examining cloze numbering logic**

I’m wondering if the system might add 1 or map -1 to a cloze number of 1, which seems unlikely because that would be invalid. It looks like the list from the backend is actually 1-based cloze numbers, so subtracting 1 in cardIndexToOrd makes sense. I'm curious why clozeNumbersInNote didn't include 3. Maybe it's due to sorting, which could misplace the value.

If the backend has sequential numbering, subtracting 1 would lead to ords of 0 and 1. This could affect how the render uses ord, possibly in a way that doesn’t reflect actual cloze number usage. Anki's internal workings may indicate that ord corresponds to the position of cloze cards rather than their actual numbers. That's a tricky detail!**Analyzing cloze card ordinals**

If I'm working with the assumption that c3 has an ord of 2, then gaps can exist in card numbering, like c1 and c3 corresponding to ords 0 and 2. If the backend returns a sequential list like [1,2], that wouldn't match the database. It's possible that anki's clozeNumbersInNote returns actual ordinals for the present cards (0,2) or their cloze numbers (1,3). 

Given this confusion, I should check the backend documentation to clarify. The comment in Collection.kt suggests it returns ords, which indicates that treating them as 0-based ordinals is the right approach. Therefore, my previous assumptions about +1 and subtracting 1 might be incorrect. Existing tests could be misleading since cardIndexToOrd returning [-1, 1] might still yield cards c1 and c3. This is an important detail to clear up!**Clarifying mapping for ordinals**

I'm pondering whether ord=-1 might correspond to c1 and ord=1 to c3, but that feels unlikely. The backend list could potentially be [1,3] for cloze numbers, unless there’s an error in the comments. To get clarity, I can write a quick unit test for Collection.clozeNumbersInNote to check what it actually returns. This way, I can set up an expectation based on the actual output.

For flexibility, I can support both ordinal representations within the code logic. If the list has ordinal+1, I'll use that; otherwise, I’ll go with the ordinal value. This approach will ensure my code remains robust no matter the backend format!**Implementing robust ordinal mapping**

I need to ensure that cardIndexToOrd accurately maps the index to the ordinal. If the list seems to have 1-based cloze numbers, I can calculate the ord with value -1. If it looks like 0-based ords, I’ll use the value as-is. I can determine this by checking the lowest value in the list: if it contains 0, I’ll treat it as ords; otherwise, as cloze numbers.

For cloze numbers, the list could include 1 or 0, so I’ll implement a robust conversion for both possibilities. To upgrade the code, I’ll modify the cardIndexToOrd function to use the sorted cloze numbers effectively and ensure it maps properly according to the rules we've established. **Summarizing the fix**

I'm working on a fix, and it involves setting the current card correctly before moving to preview. If the note type is null or the current card is null, then I'll handle the preview accordingly. Later, I will check if the current card is null and close it if needed. Okay, now I’ll summarize everything in a concise response to make sure it’s clear!

content.text:
Implemented a targeted fix in `CardTemplatePreviewer` so that when launched from the Browser → “Edit note” → “Preview”, the preview starts on the **selected card** instead of always showing the first card.

### Key changes
- **Use the provided `ordinal`** (which represents `Card.ord`) to set the initial `cardIndex` when launched from the note editor.
- For **cloze notes**, correctly map `ord` to the internal preview index (handling both backend variants where `clozeNumbersInNote()` may return cloze numbers or ordinals).
- Ensure **prev/next button enabled states** reflect the initially selected card.

### Regression tests
- Added new unit tests in **`CardTemplatePreviewerOrdinalTest.kt`** to verify:
  - Standard note types open preview on the correct (non-first) card when `ordinal` is provided.
  - Cloze notes open preview on the correct cloze card when `ordinal` is provided.

193→    fun _loadScheduler() {
194→        val ver = schedVer()
195→        if (ver == 1) {
196→            sched = DummyScheduler(this)
197→        } else {

199→                backend.setConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021, true, undoable = false)
200→            }

203→        }

211→    @Synchronized
212→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
213→        if (!dbClosed) {

224→    fun reopen(afterFullSync: Boolean = false): Boolean {
225→        Timber.i("(Re)opening Database: %s", path)
226→        return if (dbClosed) {
227→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
228→            dbInternal = db_
229→            load()
230→            _openLog()
231→            if (afterFullSync) {
232→                _loadScheduler()
233→            }
234→            created
235→        } else {
236→            false
237→        }

246→    /** Note: not in libanki.  Mark schema modified to force a full
247→     * sync, but with the confirmation checking function disabled This
248→     * is equivalent to `modSchema(False)` in Anki. A distinct method
249→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
250→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.
251→     */
252→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {
253→        db.execute(
254→            "update col set scm=?, mod=?",
255→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS(),
256→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS()
257→        )

260→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
261→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
262→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
263→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
264→     *
265→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
266→     */
267→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
268→    fun modSchema() {
269→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
270→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
271→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
272→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
273→             code to handle the user's choice */
274→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
275→        }
276→        modSchemaNoCheck()
277→    }

423→        // so not much effort was expended here
424→
425→        val noteIds = findNotes(query, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
426→        // select the card with the lowest `ord` to show
427→        return db.queryLongList(
428→            """
429→SELECT c.id
430→FROM (
431→  SELECT nid, MIN(ord) AS ord
432→  FROM cards
433→  WHERE nid IN ${Utils.ids2str(noteIds)} 
434→  GROUP BY nid
435→) AS card_with_min_ord
436→JOIN cards AS c ON card_with_min_ord.nid = c.nid AND card_with_min_ord.ord = c.ord;
437→            """.trimMargin()
438→        )
439→    }

459→        mStartReps = sched.reps

461→
462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

545→    /*
546→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
547→     */
548→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
549→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
550→        // cards without notes
551→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
555→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
556→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
557→        ) > 0
558→        // notes without cards or models
559→        if (badNotes) {
560→            return false
561→        }
562→        // invalid ords
563→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
564→            // ignore clozes
565→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
566→                continue
567→            }
568→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
569→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
570→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
571→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
572→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
573→                tmpls.length(),
574→                m.getLong("id")
575→            ) > 0
576→            if (badOrd) {
577→                return false
578→            }
579→        }
580→        return true
581→    }

583→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
584→        if (!debugLog) return
585→
586→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
587→
588→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
589→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
590→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
591→
592→        val objectsString = objects
593→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
594→            .joinToString(", ")
595→
596→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
597→    }
598→
599→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
600→        mLogHnd?.let {
601→            try {
602→                it.println(s)
603→            } catch (e: Exception) {
604→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
605→            }
606→        }
607→        Timber.d(s)
608→    }
609→
610→    private fun _openLog() {
611→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
612→        if (!debugLog) {
613→            return
614→        }
615→        try {
616→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
617→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
618→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
619→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
620→                    lpath2.delete()
621→                }
622→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
623→            }
624→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
625→        } catch (e: IOException) {
626→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
627→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
628→            debugLog = false
629→        }
630→    }
631→
632→    private fun _closeLog() {
633→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
634→        mLogHnd?.close()
635→        mLogHnd = null
636→    }

47→        return previewLayout!!.prevCard.visibility != View.GONE
48→    }
49→
50→    fun previousButtonEnabled(): Boolean {
51→        return previewLayout!!.prevCard.isEnabled && previewLayout!!.prevCard.visibility == View.VISIBLE
52→    }
53→
54→    fun nextButtonEnabled(): Boolean {
55→        return previewLayout!!.nextCard.isEnabled && previewLayout!!.nextCard.visibility == View.VISIBLE
56→    }
57→}

 43→    @Test
 44→    fun testPreviewUnsavedTemplate() {
 45→        val modelName = "Basic"
 46→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
 47→        val template = collectionBasicModelOriginal.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(0)
 48→        template.put("qfmt", template.getString("qfmt") + "PREVIEWER_TEST")
 49→        val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, collectionBasicModelOriginal)
 50→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 51→        intent.putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
 52→        intent.putExtra("index", 0)
 53→
 54→        var previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 55→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
 56→        var testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
 57→        Assert.assertTrue(
 58→            "model change did not show up?",
 59→            testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.q().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST") &&
 60→                testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.a().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST")
 61→        )
 62→
 63→        // Take it through a destroy/re-create lifecycle in order to test instance state persistence
 64→        val outBundle = Bundle()
 65→        previewerController.saveInstanceState(outBundle)
 66→        previewerController.pause().stop().destroy()
 67→        previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java).create(outBundle).start().resume().visible()
 68→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
 69→        testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
 70→        Assert.assertTrue(
 71→            "model change not preserved in lifecycle??",
 72→            testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.q().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST") &&
 73→                testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.a().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST")
 74→        )
 75→
 76→        // Make sure we can click
 77→        Assert.assertFalse("Showing the answer already?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
 78→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.disableDoubleClickPrevention()
 79→        val showAnswerButton = testCardTemplatePreviewer.findViewById<View>(R.id.preview_buttons_layout)
 80→        showAnswerButton.performClick()
 81→        Assert.assertTrue("Not showing the answer?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
 82→    }

403→    private fun assertTwoCards(testCardTemplatePreviewer: TestCardTemplatePreviewer) {
404→        assertThat("prev should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
405→        assertThat("next should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
406→
407→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onNextCard()
408→
409→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
410→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
411→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
412→
413→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onNextCard()
414→
415→        // no effect
416→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
417→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
418→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
419→
420→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onPreviousCard()
421→
422→        // previous
423→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(0))
424→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
425→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
426→    }

428→    private fun getSavedCard(notetype: NotetypeJson, ordinal: Int): Card {
429→        val n = col.newNote(notetype)
430→        val fieldNames = notetype.fieldsNames
431→        for (i in fieldNames.indices) {
432→            n.setField(i, fieldNames[i])
433→        }
434→        col.addNote(n)
435→        print(ordinal)
436→        return n.cards()[0]
437→    }
438→
439→    @KotlinCleanup("Override fieldText in constructor and remove text")
440→    private inner class Field(override val ord: Int, private val text: String) : NoteService.NoteField {
441→        override val fieldText: String
442→            get() = text
443→    }
444→}

 44→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
 45→class CardTemplateEditorTest : RobolectricTest() {
 46→    @Test
 47→    @Throws(Exception::class)
 48→    fun testEditTemplateContents() {
 49→        val modelName = "Basic"
 50→
 51→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
 52→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
 53→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 54→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
 55→        var templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 56→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 57→        var testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 58→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed yet", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 59→
 60→        // Change the model and make sure it registers as changed, but the database is unchanged
 61→        var templateFront = testEditor.findViewById<EditText>(R.id.editor_editText)
 62→        val testModelQfmtEdit = "!@#$%^&*TEST*&^%$#@!"
 63→        templateFront.text.append(testModelQfmtEdit)
 64→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 65→        assertTrue("Model did not change after edit?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 66→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
 67→
 68→        // Kill and restart the Activity, make sure model edit is preserved
 69→        val outBundle = Bundle()
 70→        templateEditorController.saveInstanceState(outBundle)
 71→        templateEditorController.pause().stop().destroy()
 72→        templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java).create(outBundle).start().resume().visible()
 73→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 74→        testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 75→        var shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
 76→        assertTrue("model change not preserved across activity lifecycle?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 77→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
 78→
 79→        // Make sure we get a confirmation dialog if we hit the back button
 80→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home))
 81→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 82→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes))
 83→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_NEGATIVE, false)
 84→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 85→        assertTrue("model change not preserved despite canceling back button?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 86→
 87→        // Make sure we things are cleared out after a cancel
 88→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home))
 89→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes))
 90→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, false)
 91→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 92→        assertFalse("model change not cleared despite discarding changes?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 93→
 94→        // Get going for content edit assertions again...
 95→        templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 96→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 97→        testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 98→        shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
 99→        templateFront = testEditor.findViewById(R.id.editor_editText)
100→        templateFront.text.append(testModelQfmtEdit)
101→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
102→        assertTrue("Model did not change after edit?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
103→
104→        // Make sure we pass the edit to the Previewer
105→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_preview))
106→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
107→        val startedIntent = shadowTestEditor.nextStartedActivity
108→        val shadowIntent = shadowOf(startedIntent)
109→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
110→        assertEquals("Previewer not started?", CardTemplatePreviewer::class.java.name, shadowIntent.intentClass.name)
111→        assertNotNull("intent did not have model JSON filename?", startedIntent.getStringExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME))
112→        assertNotEquals("Model sent to Previewer is unchanged?", testEditor.tempModel?.notetype, CardTemplateNotetype.getTempModel(startedIntent.getStringExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME)!!))
113→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
114→        shadowTestEditor.receiveResult(startedIntent, Activity.RESULT_OK, Intent())
115→
116→        // Save the template then fetch it from the collection to see if it was saved correctly
117→        val testEditorModelEdited = testEditor.tempModel?.notetype
118→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
119→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_confirm))
120→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
121→        val collectionBasicModelCopyEdited = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
122→        assertNotEquals("model is unchanged?", collectionBasicModelOriginal, collectionBasicModelCopyEdited)
123→        assertEquals("model did not save?", testEditorModelEdited.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, collectionBasicModelCopyEdited.toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
124→        assertTrue("model does not have our change?", collectionBasicModelCopyEdited.toString().contains(testModelQfmtEdit))
125→    }
