214→    /**
215→     * In a model with two card templates using different fields, some notes may only use card 1,
216→     * and some may only use card 2. If you delete the 2nd template,
217→     * it will cause the notes that only use card 2 to disappear.
218→     *
219→     * So the unit test would then be to make a model like the "basic (optional reverse card)"
220→     * with two fields Enable1 and Enable2, and two templates "card 1" and "card 2".
221→     * Both cards use selective generation, so they're empty unless the corresponding field is set.
222→     *
223→     * So then in the unit test you make the model, add the two templates, then you add two notes,
224→     * with Enable1 and Enable2 respectively set to "y".
225→     * Then you try to delete one of the templates and it should fail
226→     *
227→     * (question: but I thought deleting one should work - still one card left to maintain the note,
228→     * and second template delete should fail since we finally get to a place where no cards are left?
229→     * I am having trouble creating selectively generated cards though - I can do one optional field but not 2 ugh)
230→     *
231→     */
232→    @Test
233→    fun testDeleteTemplateWithSelectivelyGeneratedCards() {
234→        val modelName = "Basic (optional reversed card)"
235→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
236→
237→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
238→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
239→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
240→        val templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
241→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
242→        val testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
243→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed yet", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
244→        assertEquals("Model should have 2 templates now", 2, testEditor.tempModel?.templateCount)
245→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 0))
246→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 1))
247→
248→        // Try to delete Card 1 template - click delete, check confirm for card delete popup indicating it was possible, then dismiss it
249→        val shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
250→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_delete))
251→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
252→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", "Delete the “Card 1” card type, and its 0 cards?", getMaterialDialogText(true))
253→        clickMaterialDialogButton(WhichButton.NEGATIVE, true)
254→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
255→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
256→
257→        // Create note with forward and back info, Add Reverse is empty, so should only be one card
258→        val selectiveGeneratedNote = col.newNote(collectionBasicModelOriginal)
259→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(0, "TestFront")
260→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(1, "TestBack")
261→        val fields = selectiveGeneratedNote.fields
262→        for (field in fields) {
263→            Timber.d("Got a field: %s", field)
264→        }
265→        col.addNote(selectiveGeneratedNote)
266→        assertEquals("selective generation should result in one card", 1, getModelCardCount(collectionBasicModelOriginal))
267→
268→        // Try to delete the template again, but there's selective generation means it would orphan the note
269→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_delete))
270→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
271→        assertEquals(
272→            "Did not show dialog about deleting only card?",
273→            getResourceString(R.string.card_template_editor_would_delete_note),
274→            getAlertDialogText(true)
275→        )
276→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, true)
277→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
278→        assertNull("Can delete used template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
279→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
280→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 0))
281→        assertEquals("Change incorrectly added to list?", 0, testEditor.tempModel?.templateChanges?.size)
282→
283→        // Assert can delete 'Card 2'
284→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete unused template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
285→
286→        // Edit note to have Add Reverse set to 'y' so we get a second card
287→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(2, "y")
288→        selectiveGeneratedNote.flush()
289→
290→        // - assert two cards
291→        assertEquals("should be two cards now", 2, getModelCardCount(collectionBasicModelOriginal))
292→
293→        // - assert can delete either Card template but not both
294→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
295→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
296→        assertNull("Can delete both templates?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0, 1)))
297→
298→        // A couple more notes to make sure things are okay
299→        val secondNote = col.newNote(collectionBasicModelOriginal)
300→        secondNote.setField(0, "TestFront2")
301→        secondNote.setField(1, "TestBack2")
302→        secondNote.setField(2, "y")
303→        col.addNote(secondNote)
304→
305→        // - assert can delete either Card template but not both
306→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
307→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
308→        assertNull("Can delete both templates?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0, 1)))
309→    }

131→    @After
132→    @CallSuper
133→    open fun tearDown() {
134→        // If you don't clean up your ActivityControllers you will get OOM errors
135→        for (controller in mControllersForCleanup) {
136→            Timber.d("Calling destroy on controller %s", controller.get().toString())
137→            try {
138→                controller.destroy()
139→            } catch (e: Exception) {
140→                // Any exception here is likely because the test code already destroyed it, which is fine
141→                // No exception here should halt test execution since tests are over anyway.
142→            }
143→        }
144→        mControllersForCleanup.clear()
145→
146→        try {
147→            if (CollectionHelper.instance.colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
148→                CollectionHelper.instance.getColUnsafe(targetContext)!!.debugEnsureNoOpenPointers()
149→            }
150→            // If you don't tear down the database you'll get unexpected IllegalStateExceptions related to connections
151→            CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("RobolectricTest: End")
152→        } catch (ex: BackendException) {
153→            if ("CollectionNotOpen" == ex.message) {
154→                Timber.w(ex, "Collection was already disposed - may have been a problem")
155→            } else {
156→                throw ex
157→            }
158→        } finally {
159→            // After every test make sure the CollectionHelper is no longer overridden (done for null testing)
160→            disableNullCollection()
161→
162→            // called on each AnkiDroidApp.onCreate(), and spams the build
163→            // there is no onDestroy(), so call it here.
164→            Timber.uprootAll()
165→
166→            TimeManager.reset()
167→        }
168→        Dispatchers.resetMain()
169→        runBlocking { CollectionManager.discardBackend() }
170→    }

228→    // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
229→    companion object {
230→        private var mBackground = true
231→
232→        // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
233→        fun advanceRobolectricLooper() {
234→            if (!mBackground) {
235→                return
236→            }
237→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
238→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).idle()
239→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
240→        }
241→
242→        /**
243→         * * Causes all of the [Runnable]s that have been scheduled to run while advancing the clock to the start time of the last scheduled Runnable.
244→         * * Executes all posted tasks scheduled before or at the current time
245→         *
246→         * Supersedes and will eventually replace [advanceRobolectricLooper] and [advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep]
247→         */
248→        fun advanceRobolectricUiLooper() {
249→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).apply {
250→                runToEndOfTasks()
251→                idle()
252→                // CardBrowserTest:browserIsInMultiSelectModeWhenSelectingAll failed on Windows CI
253→                // This line was added and may or may not make a difference
254→                runToEndOfTasks()
255→            }
256→        }
257→
258→        // Robolectric needs some help sometimes in form of a manual kick, then a wait, to stabilize UI activity
259→        fun advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() {
260→            if (!mBackground) {
261→                return
262→            }
263→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
264→            try {
265→                Thread.sleep(500)
266→            } catch (e: Exception) {
267→                Timber.e(e)
268→            }
269→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
270→        }
271→
272→        /** This can probably be implemented in a better manner  */
273→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
274→        protected fun waitForAsyncTasksToComplete() {
275→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
276→        }

278→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
279→        protected fun <T : AnkiActivity?> startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(testClass: RobolectricTest, clazz: Class<T>?, i: Intent?): T {
280→            if (AbstractFlashcardViewer::class.java.isAssignableFrom(clazz!!)) {
281→                // fixes 'Don't know what to do with dataSource...' inside Sounds.kt
282→                // solution from https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric/issues/4673
283→                ShadowMediaPlayer.setMediaInfoProvider {
284→                    ShadowMediaPlayer.MediaInfo(1, 0)
285→                }
286→            }
287→            val controller = Robolectric.buildActivity(clazz, i)
288→                .create().start().resume().visible()
289→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
290→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
291→            testClass.saveControllerForCleanup(controller)
292→            return controller.get()
293→        }
294→    }

393→    protected fun addNoteUsingModelName(name: String?, vararg fields: String): Note {
394→        val model = col.notetypes.byName((name)!!)
395→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Could not find model '$name'")
396→        // PERF: if we modify newNote(), we can return the card and return a Pair<Note, Card> here.
397→        // Saves a database trip afterwards.
398→        val n = col.newNote(model)
399→        for ((i, field) in fields.withIndex()) {
400→            n.setField(i, field)
401→        }
402→        check(col.addNote(n) != 0) { "Could not add note: {${fields.joinToString(separator = ", ")}}" }
403→        return n
404→    }
405→
406→    protected fun addNonClozeModel(name: String, fields: Array<String>, qfmt: String?, afmt: String?): String {
407→        val model = col.notetypes.newModel(name)
408→        for (field in fields) {
409→            col.notetypes.addFieldInNewModel(model, col.notetypes.newField(field))
410→        }
411→        val t = Notetypes.newTemplate("Card 1")
412→        t.put("qfmt", qfmt)
413→        t.put("afmt", afmt)
414→        col.notetypes.addTemplateInNewModel(model, t)
415→        col.notetypes.add(model)
416→        return name
417→    }

576→    /** * A wrapper around the standard [kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest] that
577→     * takes care of updating the dispatcher used by CollectionManager as well.
578→     * * An argument could be made for using [StandardTestDispatcher] and
579→     * explicitly advanced coroutines with advanceUntilIdle(), but there are
580→     * issues with using it at the moment:
581→     * * - Any usage of CollectionManager with runBlocking() will hang. tearDown()
582→     * calls runBlocking() twice, which prevents tests from finishing.
583→     * - The hang is not limited to the scope of runTest(). Even if the runBlocking
584→     * calls in tearDown() are selectively moved into this function,
585→     * when a coroutine test fails, the next regular test
586→     * that executes after it will call runBlocking(), and it then hangs.
587→     *
588→     * A fix for this might require either wrapping all tests in runTest(),
589→     * or finding some other way to isolate the coroutine and non-coroutine tests
590→     * on separate threads/processes.
591→     * */
592→    fun runTest(
593→        context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
594→        dispatchTimeoutMs: Long = 60_000L,
595→        testBody: suspend TestScope.() -> Unit
596→    ) {
597→        Dispatchers.setMain(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())
598→        runTest(context, dispatchTimeoutMs.milliseconds) {
599→            CollectionManager.setTestDispatcher(UnconfinedTestDispatcher(testScheduler))
600→            testBody()
601→        }
602→    }
603→}

17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
18→
19→import android.content.Context
20→import android.view.View
21→import android.widget.FrameLayout
22→import android.widget.ImageView
23→import android.widget.TextView
24→import androidx.annotation.StringRes
25→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
26→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
27→import com.ichi2.anki.R
28→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
29→
30→/** The "preview" controls at the bottom of the screen for [com.ichi2.anki.Previewer] and [com.ichi2.anki.CardTemplatePreviewer] */
31→class PreviewLayout(
32→    private val buttonsLayout: FrameLayout,
33→    @VisibleForTesting val prevCard: ImageView,
34→    @VisibleForTesting val nextCard: ImageView,
35→    private val toggleAnswerText: TextView
36→) {
37→    fun displayAndInit(toggleAnswerHandler: View.OnClickListener) {
38→        buttonsLayout.visibility = View.VISIBLE
39→        buttonsLayout.setOnClickListener(toggleAnswerHandler)
40→    }
41→
42→    /** Sets the answer text to "show answer" or "hide answer" */
43→    fun setShowingAnswer(showingAnswer: Boolean) =
44→        setToggleAnswerText(if (showingAnswer) R.string.hide_answer else R.string.show_answer)
45→
46→    fun setToggleAnswerText(@StringRes res: Int) = toggleAnswerText.setText(res)
47→
48→    fun setOnPreviousCard(listener: View.OnClickListener) = prevCard.setOnClickListener(listener)
49→    fun setOnNextCard(listener: View.OnClickListener) = nextCard.setOnClickListener(listener)
50→
51→    fun setNextButtonEnabled(enabled: Boolean) = setEnabled(nextCard, enabled)
52→    fun setPrevButtonEnabled(enabled: Boolean) = setEnabled(prevCard, enabled)
53→
54→    private fun setEnabled(button: ImageView, enabled: Boolean) {
55→        button.isEnabled = enabled
56→        button.alpha = if (enabled) 1f else 0.38f
57→    }
58→
59→    fun enableAnimation(context: Context) {
60→        val resId = Themes.getResFromAttr(context, R.attr.hardButtonRippleRef)
61→        buttonsLayout.setBackgroundResource(resId)
62→        prevCard.setBackgroundResource(R.drawable.item_background_light_selectable_borderless)
63→        nextCard.setBackgroundResource(R.drawable.item_background_light_selectable_borderless)
64→    }
65→
66→    fun hideNavigationButtons() {
67→        prevCard.visibility = View.GONE
68→        nextCard.visibility = View.GONE
69→    }
70→
71→    fun showNavigationButtons() {
72→        prevCard.visibility = View.VISIBLE
73→        nextCard.visibility = View.VISIBLE
74→    }
75→
76→    companion object {
77→        fun createAndDisplay(activity: AnkiActivity, toggleAnswerHandler: View.OnClickListener): PreviewLayout {
78→            val buttonsLayout = activity.findViewById<FrameLayout>(R.id.preview_buttons_layout)
79→            val prevCard = activity.findViewById<ImageView>(R.id.preview_previous_flashcard)
80→            val nextCard = activity.findViewById<ImageView>(R.id.preview_next_flashcard)
81→            val toggleAnswerText = activity.findViewById<TextView>(R.id.preview_flip_flashcard)
82→
83→            val previewLayout = PreviewLayout(buttonsLayout, prevCard, nextCard, toggleAnswerText)
84→            previewLayout.displayAndInit(toggleAnswerHandler)
85→            if (activity.animationEnabled()) {
86→                previewLayout.enableAnimation(activity)
87→            }
88→            return previewLayout
89→        }
90→    }
91→}
